question: Will the price of Solana be above $80 on April 17?

fragility_points: One-minute settlement mechanics create path dependence and timing fragility.
Evidence is concentrated in Binance-derived data, limiting independence.
A broad crypto risk-off move before Apr 17 would quickly weaken the thesis.
key_assumptions: SOL does not suffer a roughly 6%+ drawdown into the Apr 17 12:00 ET Binance settlement minute.
Recent Binance daily closes above 80 are a useful but imperfect proxy for the narrower one-minute settlement event.
No Binance-specific pricing anomaly materially distorts the settlement candle.
main_logical_chain: Market implies 88.5% Yes from current price 0.885.
Direct Binance price checks show SOL around 85.25, giving a current buffer above the 80 strike.
Recent and medium-window Binance daily closes above 80 are about 96.7%, supporting a strong outside-view prior.
Because settlement is one exact noon-ET minute on Binance, I discount that prior for short-horizon volatility and contract narrowness.
That yields an own estimate of 84% Yes, roughly agreeing with but slightly below the market.
payload.main_thesis: SOL is already trading above $80 on Binance and recent Binance history shows it spending most days above that level, so Yes is likely, though the exact noon-ET one-minute settlement leaves meaningful residual downside risk.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
current SOLUSDT direct Binance spot check: 85.25000000
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality source-of-truth interpretation from explicit contract rules and direct Binance data, but evidence independence is only medium-low because the decisive data all come from Binance-related surfaces.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact future minute, so daily-close base rates may overstate the actual one-minute settlement probability.
Crypto can move several percent in a few days, so a short sharp selloff could still push SOL to 80 or lower at the relevant minute.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance spot check on 2026-04-14 was about 85.25, already above the 80 threshold.
Pulled Binance daily data showed 29/30 and 174/180 closes above 80, both about 96.7%.
Polymarket contract rules clearly identify Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close as the governing resolution surface.
payload.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves at 12:00 PM ET on 2026-04-17 using the Binance SOL/USDT 1-minute candle close for that exact minute.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether intraday noon-ET behavior differs materially from the daily-close base-rate proxy.
Whether any late Solana-specific catalyst emerges before settlement.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would cut the probability materially if Binance SOL/USDT trades down into the low-80s or below 80 before settlement, if a broad crypto selloff accelerates, or if late direct checks show elevated intraday downside risk around the noon ET settlement window.

fragility_points: A BTC-led or exchange-specific risk-off move could quickly erase the 6% cushion.

Binance API prices are representative of the eventual settlement surface.
SOL does not drift back toward the low-80s before the settlement minute.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves on Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on Apr 17, so exact timing and venue matter.
Current Binance spot is comfortably above 80, making Yes the base case.
But recent sub-80 history shows only a moderate drawdown is needed to flip the result.
Because no dominant scheduled catalyst was identified, ordinary crypto volatility is the main repricing path.
That supports a high but not extreme Yes probability below the market's 88.5%.
payload.main_thesis: Yes is still the base case because SOL trades materially above 80, but the market is slightly too confident because single-minute settlement risk and recent sub-80 history keep ordinary volatility relevant.
quote_anchors: The governing source of truth is Binance SOL/USDT, specifically the final Close price of the 1-minute candle for 12:00 PM ET on 2026-04-17.
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality direct evidence from the named resolution venue and explicit contract wording, with medium independence because most material evidence is Binance-centered.
strongest_disconfirmers: Binance data showed sub-80 prints earlier in April, so a downside move below the threshold is plausible.
The contract settles on one exact minute, increasing path dependence relative to a daily close.
strongest_supports: Binance SOL/USDT was around 85.31 during the run, roughly 6.2% above the strike.
Recent 1-minute prices were stable near 85.2-85.3 with no immediate threshold stress.
No dominant scheduled Solana-specific catalyst was verified before settlement.
payload.timing_relevance: The key catalyst is the shrinking time-to-settlement itself; if SOL remains above roughly 84 into Apr 16-17, Yes should stay favored, but any move toward 82-83 should force repricing because the contract settles on a single noon ET minute.
unresolved_ambiguities: No independent verification of the exact Binance front-end chart output for the settlement minute was performed.
No concrete scheduled catalyst was found, so residual risk is mostly unscheduled volatility.
payload.what_would_change_view: A verified downside catalyst before settlement, a drop back near 82 or below, or evidence of meaningful Binance UI/API mismatch would lower the Yes estimate.

That supports a Yes lean, but the remaining time gap and single-minute settlement leave meaningful downside path risk.
Therefore Yes remains more likely than No, but less confidently than the market price implies.
payload.main_thesis: SOL is more likely than not to finish above 80 on the Binance noon ET minute on April 17, but the market is too confident given remaining short-horizon crypto volatility and single-minute timing risk.
